title: India to Host BWF World Championships 2026: EOI Issued
indian_angle: India is set to host the prestigious BWF World Championships in New Delhi, a significant event for Indian badminton.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has issued an Expression of Interest for the BWF World Championships 2026, confirming India's role as the host nation for the major tournament to be held in New Delhi.

title: Tan Kim Her: India's Doubles Coach Turning Partnerships into Gold
indian_angle: This feature highlights the crucial role of India's doubles coach, Tan Kim Her, in developing successful Indian partnerships.
summary: A profile on Tan Kim Her, India's doubles coach, details his impact on transforming Indian badminton partnerships and leading them to success on the international stage.

title: PV Sindhu Exits Indonesia Open After Loss to World No. 1 An Se Young
indian_angle: India's top women's singles player, PV Sindhu, faced a tough exit from the Indonesia Open after a defeat to the formidable World No. 1.
summary: PV Sindhu's campaign at the Indonesia Open ended in the second round following a defeat to South Korea's An Se Young, extending her losing streak against the reigning Olympic champion.

title: Ayush Shetty Secures Win Against Chinese World No. 15 Weng Hong Yang
indian_angle: Young Indian shuttler Ayush Shetty delivered a notable performance by overcoming Chinese World No. 15 Weng Hong Yang.
summary: Indian shuttler Ayush Shetty advanced in his tournament, demonstrating strong form by defeating the higher-ranked Chinese player Weng Hong Yang.

title: BAI Launches Grassroot Coaches Development Program in Guwahati & Raipur
indian_angle: This program is vital for nurturing future talent and strengthening the coaching infrastructure at the grassroots level across India.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has announced a Coaches Development Program at the grassroot level, to be held in Guwahati and Raipur from June 12-21, 2026.
